column1: # Globin gene family — the textbook example of evolution by gene duplication.
# Hemoglobin alpha/beta, myoglobin, neuroglobin and cytoglobin all descend from
# one ancestral globin. In the resulting tree the sequences group by globin
# TYPE (paralog), not by species — alpha-type chains cluster together across
# human/mouse/horse, beta-type chains cluster separately, and so on — which is
# the signature of duplication-then-divergence. There is no natural reference
# row here, so this example does not use `relativeTo`.
